mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with rate declines.

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative decline in real sales exceeding 8% over three months signals a severe consumer demand contraction that forces earnings downgrades and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or out-of-sample hit rate falls below 40%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising unemployment and falling payroll employment indicates structural labor market deterioration that reduces aggregate consumption and corporate profitability, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns within one month.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) over 20-day horizons, or marginal effects of UNRATE and PAYEMS remain statistically indistinguishable from zero in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth during a monetary tightening cycle reflects robust capacity utilization and strong corporate output that supports near-term earnings momentum and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over one week.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses or becomes insignificant (t-stat < 1.5) during tightening regimes, or 5-day forward returns show negative correlation with INDPRO growth in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation exceeds 4.0% while the federal funds rate remains below 3.0%, real rates turn deeply negative, triggering aggressive Fed hiking expectations that compress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of triggered instances, or the joint threshold condition fails to separate bull and bear periods with statistical significance (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above 2.5% confirms sustained economic expansion that supports corporate revenue trajectories and equity risk premium compression, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 growth remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with GDP expansion.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate increases exceeding 0.4 percentage points within a post-shock recovery phase signal fragile labor demand that undermines consumer spending forecasts and forces equity repricing, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) during recovery regimes, or 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and real sales indicate a synchronized supply-demand breakdown that triggers inventory corrections and margin compression, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) over 5-day horizons, or marginal effects of INDPRO and RSAFS remain statistically indistinguishable from zero in multivariate regression.
